frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Seriously guys, kabhi socha hai hum log bahar kitna bhi kha le, but asli sukoon toh ghar pe Maa ke haath ke khaane mein hi milta hai. ❤️ She makes everything with so much love, never complains, aur humein realise bhi nahi hota kitni mehnat karti hain. Future mein I just hope I find someone jo aise dil se khana banaye, coz that's true love, right? #MaaKaPyaar #GharKaKhana
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's cooking and tireless effort as an act of pure, selfless love ('makes everything with so much love, never complains'), thereby erasing the labor involved. It then sets this idealized domestic service as an implicit standard for a future wife, equating 'true love' with a partner's ability to match the mother's domestic devotion and self-sacrifice, encoding the belief that a woman's love and identity are primarily expressed through domestic labor.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
